findings.claim_text: Bootstrapped by founder Edwin Chen with no outside funding, it booked *more than $1 billion in full-year 2024 revenue* — out-earning Scale's $870M on a like-for-like basis — with only ~110–130 employees and a network of ~50,000 vetted contractors.
Then Meta bought 49% of it for ~$14.3B in June 2025, valued it near $29B, and took CEO Alexandr Wang to run Meta Superintelligence Labs.
most of what is reported as "revenue" or "ARR" in this industry is gross billings, 40–70% of which is contractor pay flowing straight through to the people who actually produce the data.
